title: High-level repo map
prompt: Read-only mode. Give me a useful top-down explanation of this repository for a new contributor.
success_criteria: Covers main modules and their purpose

title: Feature ownership
prompt: Read-only mode. If I wanted to change voice behavior, which files would I inspect first and why?

title: MCP ownership
prompt: Read-only mode. Where is MCP handled in this codebase, and what parts are transport vs lifecycle management?

title: Compaction understanding
prompt: Read-only mode. Explain how Hematite handles growing conversation state and context pressure.

title: Model routing
prompt: Read-only mode. How does Hematite choose between fast vs think model paths?
success_criteria: Explains that it is heuristic and input-driven

title: No fake tools
prompt: Read-only mode. Explain Hematite's available repo-inspection tools. Use only real tool names.

title: Read-only tool selection
prompt: Read-only mode. You need to understand a feature spread across multiple files. Tell me which Hematite tools you would prefer and why.
success_criteria: Avoids mutating tools in read-only mode

title: Grounded tracing tool awareness
prompt: Read-only mode. For architecture questions, what tool should you prefer before guessing from memory?

title: LSP awareness
prompt: Read-only mode. What LSP-related tools exist in Hematite, and when are they more useful than grep?

title: Authoritative toolchain guidance
prompt: Read-only mode. Do not modify files, do not propose patches, and do not guess. I want to evaluate your tooling discipline inside this repository. Identify the best tools you actually have available for understanding a medium-sized Rust codebase like this one, explain what each is good or bad for, and give me a concrete read-only investigation plan for tracing how Hematite moves a user message from the TUI to the model and back. Do not execute the plan. Use only real tool names and end with `Best Read-Only Toolchain`.
success_criteria: Uses only real built-in tool names
Provides an ordered read-only investigation plan
Avoids fake MCP helpers and fake runtime symbols

title: Voice latency investigation planning
prompt: Read-only mode. Do not modify files, do not propose patches, and do not guess. I want to test your tool choice discipline on a non-trivial investigation. If I needed to understand why Hematite's voice output can lag behind visible text, what tools would you choose first, in order, and why? Do not execute the investigation. Use only real tool names. Distinguish between runtime/control-flow tools, file-reading tools, semantic/LSP tools, and shell verification. If a built-in authoritative tool exists for part of the question, say so. End with `Initial Investigation Order`.
success_criteria: Uses only real built-in tool names
Keeps src/ui/tui.rs and src/ui/voice.rs as likely owner files
